content.text: 01:08 Travel Delays and World Baseball Classic
shares his experience of being stuck on a plane for 9 hours in Fort Lauderdale due to storms, eventually arriving at his mother-in-law's house at 2:30 AM. (Greg Nakagawa):

describes a similar travel mishap in Miami where his hotel reservation was canceled; he discovered rooms were unavailable because fans from the Dominican Republic were in town for the World Baseball Classic semifinals.

discusses his recent service trip to the Dominican Republic, funded through a Donor Advised Fund (DAF), where his family helped build a school room and library to create empathy and connection for his children. (Shawn):
expresses interest in giving back to his alma mater and is researching if funding startups through a DAF is a viable tax-deductible strategy.
shares his goal of moving from passive to active charity work by "onboarding" his daughters (ages 23 and 25) into the Family Office to manage their foundation as a generational legacy.
discusses the formative impact of family service on children and shares various ways to contribute beyond cash, such as donating time, skills, or resources like refurbished laptops.
describes the "process pyramid" anchored in bookkeeping and document management, which allows an executive to make directional decisions rather than just administrative ones.
discuss the "pain points" of categorizing personal vs. business expenses when using tools like Monarch, especially when children or spouses use shared credit cards.
